decisions.reason: Completes the conditional offer to the Helots before the next sentence begins.
Completes the total-warfare sentence about Qing society being engineered for war.
Completes the definition of total war before the topic shifts to Qing openness.
Completes the sentence about Buddhism becoming a state religion.
Completes the dynastic sequence from Ming to Qing.
Completes the comparison that the same pattern is true for the Tang.
Completes the causal claim that after the Song, China stopped being creative.
Completes the sentence about Tang emperors not living long.
Completes the repeated down-up-down description of dynastic decline.
Completes the sentence about united elites promoting centralized national growth.
Completes the summary phrase identifying the thesis.
Completes the sentence linking massive climate change to violence.
Completes the policy result of preventing nobility from arising again.
Completes the description of localized networks not being connected nationally.
Completes the phrase that later dynasties were unified nationally.
Completes the transition that by the Tang, major cities spread out more.
Completes the regional list before the consequence sentence begins.
Completes the contrast between internal stability and wealth.
Completes the definition of a republic before its elements are explained.
Completes the claim that Rome had unusual coherence despite being spread out.
Completes the emperor's problem as the question of staying alive.
Moves the trailing starter so the contrast begins with the bureaucracy sentence.
Moves the trailing 'But' to the sentence rejecting the meritocracy claim.
Completes the assurance that top students would come from two cities.
Completes the sentence that local elites fight amongst themselves.
Completes the sentence about spending decades on the examination project.
Completes the distinction between exam success and political success.
Completes the sentence about making literacy more widespread.
Completes the legal-contract example before the rent-seeking explanation.
Completes the claim that gunpowder changes warfare and human affairs.
Completes the sentence that the inventions had no impact on Chinese society.
Completes the final causal sentence about making China poor, weak, and divided.
